question: When a Budget Entry references a hierarchical Spend Category, does it govern only direct assignments or the entire category subtree, and may parent and descendant entries coexist in one plan?
context: LEDGER now exposes stable category nodes plus exact and subtree actuals after the owner required hierarchy. BUDGET already stores Category Budget Entries, but the current flat-category contract does not define roll-up scope or prevent overlapping parent/descendant entries. Resolve before BUDGET implementation so planned and actual totals cannot double count. Candidate safe shapes are exact-node entries only, non-overlapping subtree entries only, or an explicit per-entry scope with overlap rejection.
